race.name: LoToJa Classic
race.tagline: 203 miles. Three mountain passes. Three states. Dawn in Logan, dusk beneath the Tetons. America's longest single-day USAC-sanctioned road race since 1983.

vitals.location: Logan, Utah to Teton Village, Wyoming
vitals.date: First Saturday after Labor Day annually
vitals.registration: Online at lotoja.com. Opens early spring. Licensed Race and Cyclosportive Ride classes. Solo $285, Tandem $295, Relay $185/person. Sells out. Waitlist operates first-come, first-served by category.
vitals.feed_zones: 3 crew-access feed zones + 3 neutral-only feed zones with water, bananas, gels. 50+ neutral support vehicles with wheel/mechanical service. 600+ volunteers.
vitals.cutoff_time: Must finish before dark (~8:00 PM). Most riders finish in 10-13 hours. Course record 8:18:29 (Spencer Johnson, 2018).
route_options: LoToJa Classic: 203 miles, ~9,800 ft gain — Logan, UT to Teton Village, WY (full course)
Relay: 2- or 3-person teams splitting the course at designated exchange zones

climate.primary: Mountain West early autumn — cold starts, warm valleys, exposed passes
climate.description: Early September in the northern Rockies delivers wildly variable conditions across 203 miles and three states. Pre-dawn starts in Logan see temperatures in the low 40s-50s F. Valley floors through Idaho warm to the 70s-80s F by midday. Mountain passes at 7,000+ feet can drop 20 degrees from the valley floor, with wind chill making it feel colder. The Snake River Canyon section is typically pleasant. The final stretch to Teton Village at 6,300 feet cools as afternoon fades. Rain, crosswinds, and headwinds are possible anywhere on the course — the 2021 edition saw heavy rain and strong crosswinds all day.
challenges: Temperature swings of 30-40F between pre-dawn start and midday valleys
Exposed mountain pass descents at 7,000+ feet — hypothermia risk on cold, wet years
Crosswinds and headwinds through Star Valley and Snake River Canyon
Sun exposure across long, open Idaho valley sections
Weather can change rapidly at altitude — rain and wind without warning

terrain.primary: Paved roads from valley floor through three mountain passes to Jackson Hole
terrain.surface: Entirely paved. State highways, US routes, and county roads through Utah, Idaho, and Wyoming. Road surfaces range from smooth highway to rougher rural pavement. No gravel or off-road sections. The challenge is entirely physiological — 203 miles, 9,800 feet of climbing, and three mountain passes.
features: Leg 1: Logan to Preston (30+ miles) — moderate rolling terrain through Cache Valley, warm-up miles at 4,500 ft
Leg 2: Preston to Montpelier (47 miles) — 60% climbing, including 22-mile ascent to Strawberry/Emigration Canyon summit at 7,424 ft, then descent to Bear Lake Valley
Leg 3: Montpelier to Afton (47 miles) — two major climbs: Geneva Summit (6,923 ft, 7% final grades) and Salt River Pass (7,630 ft, 6-9% final 4 miles, KOM/QOM)
Leg 4: Afton to Alpine (30 miles) — Star Valley descent and rolling terrain, recovery section before final push
Leg 5: Alpine to Teton Village (47 miles) — Snake River Canyon, Hoback Junction, final approach beneath the Teton Range to finish at 6,307 ft

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: An odyssey across three states and three mountain passes. The front half punishes you with climbing; the back half tests whether anything remains. At 203 miles, LoToJa is not about any single climb or feature — it is about the accumulation of everything across an entire day.
course_description.signature_challenge: Three mountain passes in the first 110 miles — then 90+ miles on depleted legs. The front-loaded climbing ensures that the 'flat' back half of the course is anything but easy. At mile 150, every false flat feels like a climb. At mile 180, you still have a marathon of cycling remaining. The signature challenge is not any single climb — it is the relentless accumulation of 203 miles.

logistics.airport: Salt Lake City International (SLC) — 80 miles south of Logan, 1.5 hour drive. Logan-Cache Airport (LGU) for private aviation only.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book Logan hotels for pre-race night (Best Western Plus Weston Inn, University Inn, SpringHill Suites — $80-150/night). Jackson Hole is expensive ($200-400+/night) if staying post-race. Many riders arrange return transport to Logan same evening. Book early — race weekend fills Logan hotels.
logistics.food: Pre-race dinner in Logan: multiple restaurant options along Main Street. Race morning: eat at your hotel — don't depend on finding food at 4:30 AM. Pack crew vehicle with 12+ hours of nutrition for the rider and crew food.

history.origin_story: Started in 1983 when David Bern, a Utah State University student, and Jeff Keller, owner of Sunrise Cyclery in Logan, wanted a race that resembled the difficulty of European one-day classics like Paris-Roubaix. Seven riders lined up at dawn and raced 186 miles north to Jackson. Bob VanSlyke of Logan won in just over nine hours. From those seven pioneers, LoToJa has grown to 1,500+ riders annually over 40+ years.
notable_moments: 1983: Seven riders start the first LoToJa. Bob VanSlyke wins in ~9 hours.
2018: Spencer Johnson sets current course record of 8:18:29.
2022: 40th anniversary — four decades of continuous running.
2024: Danny Van Wagoner wins the 42nd edition in a sprint finish (8:50:24), beating Adrian Rips by 6 seconds.
Women's record: Melinda MacFarlane, 9:35:00, set in 2013.
history.reputation: The longest single-day USAC-sanctioned bicycle race in America. A Mountain West institution and the defining test of one-day endurance cycling in the United States.

biased_opinion.summary: LoToJa is the American one-day classic that European cycling traditions inspired but the Mountain West made its own. Two hundred three miles from a Utah college town to the foot of the Tetons, across three states and three mountain passes, in a single day. It is not the hardest race in America. It is not the most famous. But it might be the most audacious — a race conceived in 1983 when two Logan cyclists decided that Americans deserved their own Paris-Roubaix and built it through sheer mileage.
strengths: The distance: 203 miles in a day is a statement. Finishing LoToJa earns you the right to tell the story for the rest of your life.
The journey: Three states, three mountain passes, Snake River Canyon, and a finish beneath the Grand Teton. No American one-day race covers this much geographic drama.
The crew bond: Your support vehicle drives alongside your suffering for 10-13 hours. That shared experience creates something deeper than a finisher medal.
weaknesses: Crew requirement: You cannot meaningfully do LoToJa without a dedicated support crew and vehicle. That is a significant logistical and social ask.
Point-to-point logistics: Your car is 200 miles behind you at the finish. Getting home is its own adventure.
Geographic isolation: This is a Mountain West event. If you do not live in Utah, Idaho, or Wyoming, the travel logistics compound the already-complex race logistics.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want to test the outer boundary of what you can accomplish on a bicycle in a single day, LoToJa is the answer. 203 miles. Three states. Three mountain passes. A finish beneath the Tetons. There is nothing else like it in America.

final_verdict.one_liner: America's longest one-day road race. Three states. Three passes. One unforgettable day.
final_verdict.should_you_race: LoToJa is for riders who want more than a century ride — who want a genuine odyssey. If you can sustain moderate effort for 10-13 hours, have a reliable crew, and want a finishing story that few other events can match, this is your race. But be honest with yourself: 203 miles is a very long day, and the three mountain passes in the first half ensure you will face the final 90 miles on tired legs.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want ultra-distance without the crew logistics: try RAGBRAI (supported, 7-day format across Iowa). If you want a similar mountain experience in a shorter package: try the Assault on Mt. Mitchell (102 miles, 11,000 ft climbing). If you want the European one-day classic vibe that inspired LoToJa: try Paris-Roubaix Challenge (pave, prestige, pain).

citations.label: Alto Velo Racing Club — 2024 LoToJa Race Report
Salt Lake Magazine — Tackling the 203-mile LoToJa

videos.title: Preparing for LOTOJA - First Ride of the Year with TEAM ONWATCH
videos.description:
"It's not just a hill, it's an opportunity." Sardine Canyon summit of 5,899 feet offered just that to the members of Team OnWatch March 19, 2021. With temperatures in the low 60's, they headed out for a ride around the Wellsville mountain range. What a great day to start out the season. 

Team OnWatch on Facebook:  https://www.facebook.com/loganraceclub
Team OnWatch Website: https://www.facebook.com/loganraceclub/
Tyler on Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/tylervanderbeek
Blog: https://www.ourlifeelevated.com/

videos.title: What its like to ride with LOTOJA winners
videos.description:
I was invited to join a group ride not knowing who was coming for sure and how fast we would ride.  Turned out to be a fast training ride with several winners of LOTOJA in the group.  
Super awesome day & group!
I love riding with faster people to help me get stronger.

If you're wondering what LOTOJA is, click https://lotoja.com/

Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/tylerbeek

Music: Bensound.com Birth of a Hero

videos.curation_reason: First-person training ride with LOTOJA winners showing course conditions and race preparation insights.

quotes.text: There's always a headwind in the morning of this canyon so hopefully I didn't miss the group and have to do it by myself. Made it to the top of blacksmith fourth canyon nice and easy ride they said until this guy maybe all these guys decided hey let's just hammer it up the hills turned into a bit of a race.

rider_intel.search_text: The available transcripts contain limited race-specific information about LoToJa Classic. One video captures pre-race atmosphere with riders carb-loading on Friday night and gathering at the park before the event. There are mentions of support teams and crew cars, with references to Alpine as a stop location during the race. The transcripts show family participation including a tandem rider team, and community support with crew vehicles carrying multiple team members. However, the transcripts lack detailed course information, specific terrain descriptions, gear recommendations, or pacing strategies that would be typical race intelligence. Most content focuses on local training rides in northern Utah canyons rather than the actual LoToJa race route from Logan to Jackson.
